prompt: If your writing style were a material (wood, brass, cotton, glass, concrete, etc.), which would it be and why?
response: I would compare my writing style to porcelain. It is elegant and refined, like porcelain, yet strong and durable. Just as porcelain can be molded into various shapes and designs, I can adapt my writing style to different contexts and topics. The smoothness of the material represents the fluidity and coherence of my texts, while its transparency reflects the clarity and straightforwardness of my thoughts and ideas. Like porcelain, my writing style has the ability to be both functional and decorative, catering to the needs of the audience while also engaging their senses.
